executiveSummary.text: Regime: AI-led risk-on, but constrained by oil and real rates. S&P 500 closed at 7,501.24 (+0.77%), Nasdaq at 26,635.22 (+0.88%), VIX at 17.26 (-3.41%), while 10Y Treasury yield was 4.47% and 10Y real yield 2.00% on May 14. Scenario weights: 55% continued AI-led melt-up/chop, 30% rates/oil-driven multiple compression, 15% policy/geopolitical air pocket. Confidence: medium-high.

AI scarcity thesis strengthened in the last 24h: CNBC/Yahoo reported Nvidia rose more than 4% after H200 sales approvals to several Chinese firms, while Cerebras surged nearly 70% in its debut. 2nd order: AI demand remains capital-market-funded and supply-constrained; 3rd order: valuation risk rises as private/IPO comps reprice the whole semiconductor complex. Confidence: medium.

Oil remains the macro swing factor, not a simple 'risk-off = buy gold' story. WTI was about $102/bbl and OilPrice showed fresh Hormuz disruption headlines within 7-16 hours. Chain: oil shock -> inflation impulse -> Fed cuts delayed / real yields stay high -> long-duration QQQ and REIT multiples face pressure; gold only benefits if real yields fall, not merely if headlines worsen. Confidence: high on causal chain, medium on timing.

Singapore sleeve is stable but rate-sensitive. STI closed at 4,995.94 (-0.16%); DBS and OCBC were modestly positive on Yahoo's related-ticker panel, while fresh bank commentary highlighted that falling rates would pressure NII even as wealth/non-interest income helps. Confidence: medium.

Fed credibility/rate path remains the portfolio's hidden beta. Fresh Yahoo headlines showed Miran stepping down from the Fed Board, paving the way for Kevin Warsh as Fed chair; Treasury data still shows 10Y real yields near 2%. 2nd order: a more dovish Fed narrative may lift equities initially; 3rd order: if bond markets price inflation risk/term premium, both AI duration and REITs can derate despite dovish headlines. Confidence: medium.

usGlobal.what: Yahoo Finance/CNBC reported US stocks hit fresh records as the AI trade roared back; Nvidia rose more than 4% after reports that the US approved H200 chip sales to several Chinese firms.
usGlobal.why: First order: China-related revenue optionality and AI demand sentiment improve. Second order: more H200 access can extend Nvidia's near-term order book and support TSM/semicap demand. Third order: policy volatility cuts both ways — approvals can be reversed or narrowed, creating gap risk in semis.
usGlobal.implication: Base case 55%: stay invested and let winners compound; 30%: wait for pullbacks because rates/oil cap multiples; 15%: export-control reversal causes a fast semiconductor drawdown. Do not chase at open unless breadth confirms.

usGlobal.what: Cerebras surged nearly 70% in the biggest IPO of 2026, according to Yahoo Finance, with Brave/CNBC snippets also showing a $95B market cap narrative around the AI-chip debut.
usGlobal.why: First order: public markets are funding AI accelerators aggressively. Second order: high IPO valuations lift sentiment for AI suppliers and capital equipment. Third order: if concept-stage AI valuations overheat, quality leaders may still rise but downside beta increases when the narrative cracks.
usGlobal.implication: Confirmation of scarcity thesis, not a green light to add indiscriminately. Prefer existing quality picks-and-shovels on volatility rather than chasing IPO sympathy.

usGlobal.what: Yahoo's front page carried a fresh headline that Miran will step down from the Fed Board, paving the way for Kevin Warsh as Fed chair; Brave also surfaced current Fed-rate coverage with the Fed funds range at 3.5%-3.75%.
usGlobal.why: First order: Fed leadership uncertainty changes rate-cut expectations. Second order: if markets view the Fed as more dovish while inflation remains sticky, term premium can rise rather than fall. Third order: higher real discount rates pressure QQQ/AI duration, REITs and gold despite nominally dovish headlines.
usGlobal.implication: Watch 10Y real yield and 30Y term premium, not just Fed-cut headlines. A real-yield break below ~1.85% would help REITs/gold; a push above ~2.10% raises de-risking odds.

singapore.what: STI closed at 4,995.94 (-0.16%) at 5:20pm SGT; Yahoo's related panel showed DBS at 60.13 (+0.38%) and OCBC at 22.95 (+0.26%).
singapore.why: First order: SG banks remain resilient while local index momentum pauses near 5,000. Second order: higher global real yields support bank NII but pressure REIT cap rates. Third order: KL's SG dividend sleeve may keep providing ballast even if REITs lag until yields roll over.
singapore.implication: Keep SG dividend ballast; add REITs only on yield-spread discipline, not because they look optically cheap.

singapore.what: Brave surfaced fresh Singapore-bank commentary within 19 hours noting DBS and OCBC near all-time highs while UOB lags, with the key issue being lower-rate pressure on NII versus resilient loan growth and non-interest income.
singapore.why: First order: lower rates would compress bank margins. Second order: wealth fees/non-interest income can offset part of NII pressure. Third order: if rates fall because growth weakens rather than inflation cools, banks and REITs may both struggle, so the reason for lower yields matters.
singapore.implication: Do not sell bank winners solely on rate-cut fear; monitor NII guidance and fee-income mix. UOB lag may be an eventual relative-value watch, not an immediate trade.

singapore.what: Brave/Investing.com showed Eneos buying Chevron Asia assets including Singapore Refining Company exposure for about $2.17B within the last 19 hours.
singapore.why: First order: strategic value of Singapore-linked energy/refining assets remains high while oil is elevated. Second order: energy security and refining/logistics assets can benefit from regional supply-chain complexity. Third order: persistent oil strength supports energy infrastructure cash flows but also keeps inflation and rates high, hurting REIT duration.
singapore.implication: Positive read-through for Singapore infrastructure scarcity, but macro transmission is mixed because oil-led inflation is not automatically equity-positive.

commodities.what: OilPrice showed WTI around $102.0 (+0.78%) and Brent around $105.7 (+0.09%), plus fresh headlines on Hormuz-related shipping disruptions within 7-16 hours.
commodities.why: First order: higher oil feeds headline inflation and freight/energy costs. Second order: Fed cuts become harder and real yields stay elevated. Third order: AI/growth multiples and REITs derate; gold can underperform cash if real rates rise despite geopolitical stress.
commodities.implication: Treat oil as the main macro risk switch. A sustained WTI >$105 with rising 10Y real yields is a de-risk signal for tactical AI adds.

commodities.what: Yahoo showed gold around $4,662/oz (-0.5%) while Treasury 10Y real yield was 2.00% on May 14.
commodities.why: First order: gold is being priced against real rates, not simply war headlines. Second order: if oil keeps inflation sticky, the Fed cannot validate lower real rates. Third order: gold's zero-yield opportunity cost rises versus cash/T-bills, so crisis headlines can coincide with weaker gold.
commodities.implication: Hold strategic metals, but do not add gold on geopolitics alone. Add only if real yields fall or if gold sells off while real yields stop rising.

commodities.what: Fresh market snippets noted silver/copper strength relative to gold, while AI/data-center demand continues to support the metals scarcity narrative.
commodities.why: First order: copper/silver are more cyclical and electrification-linked than gold. Second order: AI grid buildout raises physical demand but higher rates can cap speculative multiples. Third order: a growth scare would hit copper before gold; an AI capex acceleration with stable rates would favor copper/silver over gold.
commodities.implication: Keep metals diversified: gold for real-rate hedge, copper/silver for AI electrification beta. Do not treat all metals as one trade.

narratives.title: AI chip reopening meets export-control volatility
narratives.description: H200 approvals and Trump-Xi summit optics support semis today, but the same policy channel can reverse quickly. Probability: 50% incremental easing/managed access, 35% status quo, 15% renewed restriction shock. Confidence: medium.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance/CNBC live market updates within last 24h
narratives.priceImpact: Positive near term; negative convexity if policy reverses

narratives.title: AI IPO window reopens
narratives.description: Cerebras' near-70% debut shows public-market appetite for AI compute assets. 2nd order: easier capital formation for AI infrastructure; 3rd order: bubble-risk narrative rises as comps inflate.

narratives.title: Oil is the real Fed constraint
narratives.description: Hormuz/oil headlines matter through inflation and real rates, not through a generic safe-haven channel. Base case 45% oil stays elevated but contained; 35% de-escalation; 20% escalation above $110 WTI. Confidence: medium.
narratives.source: OilPrice live prices/headlines within 2-18h; Yahoo market strip
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration if real yields rise; mixed for banks; conditional for gold

narratives.title: Singapore banks as ballast, REITs as duration
narratives.description: STI is near 5,000 with banks firm, but REITs remain a real-yield trade. Lower rates help REITs only if they reflect easing inflation, not growth stress or Fed credibility loss.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance Singapore quote page and Brave bank commentary within 19h
narratives.priceImpact: Banks stable; REITs wait for real-yield relief

secondOrderEffects.text: H200 China approvals -> Nvidia revenue optionality improves (1st) -> TSM/advanced packaging/memory/equipment demand expectations rise (2nd) -> portfolio concentration and export-policy gap risk rise because the same policy lever can reverse (3rd).
secondOrderEffects.implication: Stay exposed to AI infra but avoid adding all at once after policy-driven rallies.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil/Hormuz disruption -> headline inflation and freight costs rise (1st) -> Fed cuts delayed and real yields stay around/above 2% (2nd) -> gold can fall versus cash and REIT/QQQ multiples can compress (3rd).
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not buy gold or REITs solely on geopolitical stress; watch real yields.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI IPO strength -> capital available for chip challengers (1st) -> hyperscalers gain bargaining leverage and optionality (2nd) -> incumbent GPU margins/valuation assumptions may face longer-run scrutiny even if near-term demand is robust (3rd).
secondOrderEffects.implication: Quality incumbents remain core, but track gross-margin and capex-efficiency signals.

secondOrderEffects.text: Fed leadership dovishness -> equity multiple relief initially (1st) -> bond market may demand higher inflation/credibility term premium (2nd) -> higher long real rates hurt duration assets despite dovish rhetoric (3rd).
secondOrderEffects.implication: Use Treasury real yields as the truth signal; not Fed headlines.

investment.thesis: Keep AI infrastructure core; add only on volatility in semicap/equipment and quality hyperscaler suppliers.
investment.trigger: Add in tranches if NVDA/semicap basket pulls back 7-10% while H200/export-policy news remains constructive and 10Y real yield stays below ~2.10%.
investment.whyNow: Fresh H200 approval and Cerebras IPO reinforce demand, but price has already moved.
investment.invalidator: Export approvals reversed, hyperscaler capex guidance cut, or 10Y real yield sustainably above 2.25%.

investment.thesis: Maintain SG bank ballast; be patient on REIT duration exposure.
investment.trigger: Add REITs only if spreads widen materially or 10Y real yield breaks below ~1.85% because inflation is cooling, not because growth is cracking.
investment.whyNow: STI is stable and banks firm, but REITs remain rate-sensitive.
investment.invalidator: Singapore bank NII guidance deteriorates faster than fee income offsets, or REIT refinancing spreads widen.

trading.thesis: Avoid chasing the AI gap; use oil/real-yield thresholds as tactical risk switch.
trading.trigger: If WTI holds >$105 and 10Y real yield rises above 2.10%, pause AI adds or trim short-term trading exposure; if WTI falls below $95 with real yields stable/falling, re-open tactical adds.
trading.whyNow: Oil is the fastest transmission path from geopolitics to Fed pricing to AI multiples.
trading.invalidator: Oil spike reverses without inflation expectations rising, or AI breadth expands despite higher real yields.

trading.thesis: Relative-value watch: UOB lag versus DBS/OCBC may become attractive if rate and fee-income data stabilize.
trading.trigger: Watch next bank updates for resilient loan growth and wealth fees; act only if UOB underperformance widens without a fundamental downgrade.
trading.whyNow: Fresh commentary flags DBS/OCBC near highs and UOB lagging.
trading.invalidator: UOB ASEAN credit quality or NII outlook deteriorates relative to peers.

watchlist.thesis: Gold add requires falling real yields, not scarier headlines.
watchlist.trigger: Watch 10Y real yield below ~1.85% or gold holding support while real yields stop rising.
watchlist.whyNow: Gold is down while oil/geopolitical risk is high, confirming real-rate sensitivity.
watchlist.invalidator: 10Y real yield climbs above 2.15% and gold fails to hold trend.

watchlist.thesis: Copper/silver/grid assets remain the cleaner AI-electrification scarcity expression if growth holds.
watchlist.trigger: Add only if AI capex breadth continues and copper/silver consolidate without a growth-scare signal.
watchlist.whyNow: AI power/data-center demand supports physical scarcity, but higher rates can still hit cyclicals.
watchlist.invalidator: Global PMI/growth data weakens or AI capex guidance slows.

riskWatch.name: Oil inflation -> real-rate shock
riskWatch.description: WTI around $102 with fresh Hormuz disruption headlines. If oil rises further, inflation expectations and real yields can rise together, hitting AI duration, REITs and gold.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash buffer; delay tactical AI adds above WTI $105 plus 10Y real yield >2.10%; avoid treating gold as automatic crisis hedge.

riskWatch.name: AI valuation/IPO heat
riskWatch.description: Cerebras' near-70% debut validates AI appetite but also raises bubble-risk framing. Momentum can continue, but downside beta increases when narratives get crowded.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prefer profitable AI infrastructure leaders; add in tranches; avoid low-quality sympathy trades.

riskWatch.name: Fed credibility / term premium
riskWatch.description: Fed leadership transition may be read as dovish, but bonds can react by pricing higher inflation risk and term premium.
riskWatch.mitigation: Track 10Y/30Y and TIPS real yields; favor banks/cash ballast if real yields rise.

riskWatch.name: Export-control reversal
riskWatch.description: Nvidia H200 approval headlines are positive, but policy-driven semiconductor rallies can reverse quickly if US-China talks sour.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not concentrate new buys into one policy headline; diversify across equipment, memory, foundry, grid and cash.

keyEvents.text: Trump-Xi summit follow-through: watch for concrete language on tariffs, export controls and China access for advanced AI chips.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance/CNBC Asia live updates within last 24h

keyEvents.text: Oil/Hormuz shipping headlines over the next 24-48h; WTI >$105 would increase inflation/real-yield pressure.
Treasury real yields: 10Y TIPS at 2.00% on May 14; next move determines whether gold/REITs get relief or further pressure.
keyEvents.source: US Treasury daily yield and real-yield curve, May 14

keyEvents.text: Fed leadership headlines around Miran/Warsh and market pricing of rate cuts versus term premium.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance headline within 5h; Brave Fed search within 24h

bottomLine.whatMatters: AI demand is still real and market-supported, but the decisive macro variable is oil -> inflation -> real yields. The portfolio's risk assets can rise with AI earnings, but they are vulnerable if WTI and real yields rise together.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Stay invested in the scarcity thesis, keep cash optionality, add AI infrastructure only on pullbacks, and use 10Y real yield/WTI as the decision dashboard. Keep SG banks as ballast; wait for real-yield relief before aggressive REIT or gold adds.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore simplistic 'war = buy gold' or 'Fed chair dovish = all assets up' narratives. The transmission path matters: real rates and term premium decide gold, REITs and AI multiples.
